repo_prior_art.cite: doc/API.md Section 3: ExactID / PartialID catalogues are empty; Q1 panel entries are binary-history regression results

published_prior_art.cite: LeGruyer2009 + classical C^{1,1} Whitney extension theory: finite value-derivative compatibility and minimal Lipschitz extension are known; DID moment rows are the application-specific part
none; closest verified papers give continuous-DID point-ID/no-stayer estimators or smoothness bounds, not a sharp DID-sourced derivative support-gap envelope
DHaultfoeuilleHoderleinSasaki2023 and deChaisemartinDHaultfoeuillePasquierSowVazquezBare2022: point-ID continuous-DID ACR/slope limits under their support primitives

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The literature checklist omits the closest mathematical prior art on C^{1,1} Whitney extension/minimal Lipschitz derivative extension, especially Le Gruyer 2009 and Wells-style Whitney extension results.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The zero-gap recovery is a sanity reduction to published continuous-DID point-ID results, not a novel theorem.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship; as written the kernel looks like applying known C^{1,1} Whitney/smoothness envelopes to DID moment rows, not a named open-regime resolution with a cited open problem.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The stated W_T constraints are not sufficient for a C^{1,1} curve with Lipschitz constant B: with x0=0,x1=1,m=(0,1/2),v=(0,0),B=1 the rows pass, but no derivative with endpoints 0 and Lipschitz constant 1 can integrate to 1/2.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The claimed two-endpoint Whitney modulus is built from the same incomplete Taylor rows; it is missing the sharp integral/Le Gruyer compatibility condition needed for a necessary-and-sufficient C^{1,1} derivative envelope.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 2
soundness_flags.one_line: Intervals merely shrinking to μ(s_n) do not identify μ'(d); without a rate such as width(I_s)=o(|s-d|) and suitable side/support conditions, feasible curves can keep different derivatives at d while matching shrinking nearby intervals.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the pairwise Taylor rows by a correct one-dimensional C^{1,1} Whitney/Le Gruyer compatibility system, then restate Theorem 1 as a projection over that feasible set.
Strengthen zero-gap recovery with explicit interval-shrinkage rates relative to |s-d|, plus one-sided/two-sided support conditions sufficient to force derivative equality.

Add Whitney-extension prior art to Related work and explain why the new object is not the extension theorem itself but the causal sharpness/pasting of DID moment intervals into a full potential-outcome law.

To target flagship, name a precise open limitation in the no-stayer continuous-DID literature and add a regime-opening result beyond smoothness interpolation, such as a sharp computable formula plus inference or a strict extension over a published nonparametric bound class.

recommended_next_step: Major redraft: repair the C^{1,1} compatibility and zero-gap theorem first, then re-run novelty review against Whitney-extension and continuous-DID prior art.
